global.title: 5 things to know for May 7: Hantavirus outbreak, peace talks, market rebound, small businesses, White House ballroom - CNN
global.summary: A cruise ship outbreak linked to hantavirus has left passengers still aboard as officials trace contacts, while Iran is expected to respond to a U.S. proposal to end the war.
global.whyItMatters: This combines public-health concern and fragile peace efforts—two areas where pastors may need to pray, speak calmly, and care for anxious people.

global.title: Morning Bid: No stopping AI frenzy in Asia - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters says markets are watching both the AI-driven rally in Asia and the possibility of a peace deal with Iran, even as oil remains elevated and the Strait of Hormuz crisis is unresolved.
global.whyItMatters: Economic volatility shapes household stress, generosity, and congregational needs; this helps frame pastoral awareness of financial pressure.

canadian.title: Airbus wins $19bn order in Canada’s biggest ever aircraft deal - Financial Times
canadian.summary: Airbus has secured what the FT describes as Canada’s biggest-ever aircraft deal, signaling a major development for the country’s aviation and industrial sectors.
canadian.whyItMatters: Large economic moves can affect jobs, regional confidence, and family stability—useful context for pastoral leadership in uncertain times.

canadian.title: U.S. Private Sector Added 109,000 Jobs In April—As Small Businesses Boost Growth - Forbes
canadian.summary: Forbes reports April job growth was led by education, health services, and small businesses, with mid-sized firms showing more weakness.
canadian.whyItMatters: Employment trends often show up first in prayer requests, benevolence needs, and the emotional tone of a church community.

persecutedChurch.title: Nun assaulted in Jerusalem as concern grows over attacks on Christians - The Catholic Herald
persecutedChurch.summary: After the assault of a nun in Jerusalem, police arrested a suspect and said they are treating the case seriously, while Christian leaders warn the attack reflects a wider pattern of hostility.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct reminder to pray for vulnerable believers and to keep religious freedom and Christian witness before the congregation.

persecutedChurch.title: For Christians in Israel and Jerusalem, intolerance is becoming normal - Al Jazeera
persecutedChurch.summary: Christians in Israel and East Jerusalem say harassment, intimidation, and assaults are becoming more routine, with local monitors documenting repeated incidents against clergy and church property.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Pastors can help their people see persecution not as abstract news but as a present burden on fellow believers in the land of Jesus’ ministry.

leadership.title: Why workforce wellbeing can no longer sit on the sidelines in ECEC - The Sector
leadership.summary: This piece argues that wellbeing should be built into leadership, systems, and organizational design rather than treated as a late response to burnout.
leadership.whyItMatters: Healthy ministry culture is sustained by intentional systems, not just good intentions; this is a useful lens for staff care and volunteer leadership.

goodNews.title: Children's Theatre Company performing "Wizard of Oz" this summer - CBS News
goodNews.summary: A children’s production of The Wizard of Oz is bringing a timeless story of friendship to families this summer.
goodNews.whyItMatters: Stories of beauty, friendship, and shared community life offer a welcome note of joy pastors can carry into conversations and gatherings.
